spot_checks_performed: raw_response inspection for all 4 model probes
Nemotron-mini-4b identity vs classification capability check

findings.re_test_methodology: Verified as clean-slate. Probes in artifacts/iterations/0.2.15/vetting/ contain unique timestamps and wall-clock data specific to the 0.2.15 environment. No evidence of recycled 0.2.13 W2.5 results found.
findings.retain_decision_glm: Pass. Evidence (<|begin_of_box|> token leakage) matches characterization. Timeout and schema issues from 0.2.13 are resolved on fixed dispatcher. Recommendation to RETAIN as Auditor candidate is defensible.
findings.retain_decision_nemotron: Pass. Classify probe returns 'bug' correctly, resolving feature-bias. Identity failure ('BERT') is documented and correctly used to downgrade classification to 'compromised' while maintaining functional utility for triage.
findings.dispatcher_integrity: W1.5 repairs (/api/chat, 32K context, Qwen stop tokens) are intact. Llama 3.2 integration is model-agnostic and does not regress existing paths.
findings.cross_project_contamination: Zero instances found. 11-pillar count verified in tier1-roster-validation-0.2.15.md. No kjtcom terminology leakage.

recommendation: W0 validated. W1 Ollama Tier 1 capability audit may proceed. GLM and Nemotron retained in operational roster for W4 cross-model cascade consideration.
